title: CVE-2025-30066: tj-actions/changed-files GitHub Action Compromised via Renovate Bot Impersonation to Expose Secrets in 23,000 Repositories
description: Attackers compromised the tj-actions/changed-files GitHub Action (CVE-2025-30066) by spoofing a commit as the legitimate renovate[bot] using a stolen GitHub PAT, injecting a base64-encoded Node.js payload that downloaded a malicious Python script scanning Runner Worker memory for secrets and printing them in workflow logs of over 23,000 affected repositories.
label: AV-410

title: CVE-2025-30154: reviewdog/action-setup GitHub Action Compromised Enabling Chain Attack on tj-actions Organization
description: The reviewdog/action-setup GitHub Action was compromised (CVE-2025-30154) enabling a chained supply chain attack where stolen credentials propagated from reviewdog to the tj-actions organization, demonstrating how a single GitHub Action compromise can cascade through interconnected action ecosystems.
label: AV-410

title: spotbugs/sonar-findbugs pull_request_target Workflow Leaks PAT Enabling tj-actions Compromise Chain
description: A malicious pull request submitted to spotbugs/sonar-findbugs exploited a pull_request_target workflow trigger that used a maintainer PAT as a secret, leaking the token that was subsequently used to invite a malicious actor to the spotbugs organization and initiate the chain of compromises ending with the tj-actions/changed-files supply chain attack.
label: AV-410

title: Coinbase Targeted via tj-actions/changed-files CVE-2025-30066 Compromise Affecting 70,000 Customers
description: Attackers specifically targeted Coinbase through the tj-actions/changed-files supply chain attack, leveraging exposed secrets to access Coinbase CI/CD pipelines in an attack that ultimately affected approximately 70,000 Coinbase customers and highlighted how targeted supply chain attacks can cause widespread collateral damage.
label: AV-410

title: Ultralytics GitHub Actions Cache Poisoning via pull_request_target Enables Malicious PyPI Package Publication
description: The Ultralytics YOLO repository suffered a supply chain attack where shell injection through a pull_request_target trigger was combined with GitHub Actions cache poisoning to gain access to the PyPI publishing pipeline, resulting in the publication of malicious Ultralytics package versions to PyPI.
label: AV-410

title: Kong Ingress Controller GitHub Actions Workflow Bypassed via Dependabot Impersonation Enabling PAT Exfiltration
description: The Kong Ingress Controller's GitHub Actions workflow restricted pull_request_target execution to trusted users but was bypassed through Dependabot impersonation, allowing attackers to exfiltrate a GitHub PAT and use it to compromise downstream dependencies.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud npm Worm Automatically Propagates Malicious Postinstall Scripts via Compromised npm Tokens Across Packages
description: The Shai-Hulud malware demonstrated worm-like behavior by using compromised npm tokens to automatically publish malicious postinstall scripts to multiple npm packages and compromised GitHub tokens to inject malicious GitHub Actions workflows, creating self-propagating supply chain infections across interconnected packages.
label: AV-410

title: npm debug chalk and color Package Maintainer Phished via Fake 2FA Update Email Enabling Account Takeover
description: Maintainers of the widely-used npm packages debug, chalk, and color were targeted by phishing emails impersonating GitHub's 2FA update process, tricking maintainers with TOTP 2FA enabled into surrendering their credentials and enabling attackers to take over the accounts and publish malicious package versions.
label: AV-200

title: polyfill.io Supply Chain Attack via CDN Domain Purchase Delivers Malicious JavaScript to 100,000+ Websites
description: Attackers purchased the polyfill.io CDN domain and GitHub organization after a control handoff, redirecting the widely-used JavaScript polyfill service to deliver malicious scripts to over 100,000 websites that had embedded the CDN link, demonstrating the risk of third-party CDN dependency in web supply chains.
label: AV-509

title: Lazarus Group Operation 99 Targets Web3 and Cryptocurrency Developers via Fake Job Repository Supply Chain Attack
description: The Lazarus Group's Operation 99 campaign targeted Web3 and cryptocurrency developers by creating fake job offer repositories that contained malicious code executed during CI/CD evaluation, exfiltrating developer credentials and private keys from compromised development environments.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud Malware Downloads TruffleHog Secret Scanner to Systematically Find and Exfiltrate All Developer Secrets
description: The Shai-Hulud supply chain malware downloaded the legitimate TruffleHog secret scanning tool after installation, using it to systematically scan developer file systems and git histories for all committed secrets, API keys, and credentials before exfiltrating the comprehensive results to attacker infrastructure.
label: AV-400

title: Koi Security Discovers Malicious VS Code Extensions on OpenVSX Marketplace Harvesting Developer Secrets
description: Koi Security discovered malicious VS Code extensions distributed through the OpenVSX marketplace that harvested developer secrets, SSH keys, and authentication tokens from developer workstations, demonstrating the expanding attack surface of IDE extension marketplaces as supply chain vectors.
label: AV-200

title: GitHub Actions Mutable Version Tags Enable Instant Compromise of All Dependent Workflows Upon Tag Override
description: Security researchers documented a fundamental design vulnerability in GitHub Actions where mutable version tags allow attackers who compromise an action repository to instantly compromise every workflow depending on that action by overriding existing tags to point to malicious commits, unlike immutable package systems used by Go and other ecosystems.
label: AV-410

title: Compromised GitHub Actions Workflows Bypass Trusted Publishing by Stealing OIDC Tokens During Legitimate Workflow Execution
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks where compromised GitHub Actions workflows steal OpenID Connect tokens during legitimate workflow execution, bypassing trusted publishing security controls by obtaining OIDC tokens generated for authorized publishing steps and using them to authenticate malicious package uploads.
label: AV-410

title: GitHub npm Scanning Detects Hundreds of Malicious Package Versions Daily Confirming Industrial Scale of Supply Chain Attacks
description: GitHub's npm malware scanning program confirmed that hundreds of newly published npm package versions contain malicious code daily, with human review confirming true positives before action is taken, demonstrating that typosquatting and package compromise have reached industrial scale in the npm ecosystem.
label: AV-200

title: MavenGate Vulnerability Affects Dependencies of Google Facebook Signal and Amazon Applications via Expired GroupIds
description: The MavenGate attack demonstrated that dependencies used by applications from Google, Facebook, Signal, and Amazon were vulnerable to groupId hijacking via expired domain purchases, with researchers finding 6,170 of 33,938 analyzed Maven groupId domains available for purchase enabling silent injection of malicious code.
label: AV-509

title: MavenGate Enables Supply Chain Attacks Targeting Google Play Store Android Applications via Abandoned Maven GroupIds
description: The MavenGate supply chain attack method specifically threatened Android applications distributed through the Google Play Store, where expired Maven groupIds used by popular Android libraries could be purchased by attackers to inject malicious code into apps affecting millions of end users.
label: AV-509

title: Cl0p Ransomware Gang Exploits Oracle E-Business Suite Zero-Day CVE-2025-61882 in Supply Chain Extortion Campaign
description: The Cl0p ransomware gang exploited a zero-day vulnerability in Oracle E-Business Suite (CVE-2025-61882) in a supply chain extortion campaign discovered by Google and Mandiant, stealing private files from victims including NHS England, Schneider Electric, Harvard University, American Airlines, and The Washington Post.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in AI Model Training Scripts Enable Hidden Data Poisoning of Machine Learning Models
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks embedded in AI model training scripts where hidden Unicode characters alter data preprocessing and label assignment logic, enabling silent data poisoning that causes trained models to exhibit hidden malicious behaviors invisible during training code review.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Solidity Smart Contracts Enable Hidden Token Theft Logic Invisible in Code Review
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Solidity smart contracts where hidden Unicode bidirectional characters alter conditional logic and token transfer permissions, enabling hidden fund theft mechanisms that appear as legitimate token management code during smart contract security audits.
label: AV-300

title: Malicious Hugging Face Model Repository Typosquats Popular AI Models to Distribute Pickle-Based Malware
description: Malicious Hugging Face model repositories used typosquatted names mimicking popular AI models including GPT and BERT variants, distributing malicious model files containing pickle-based payloads that executed arbitrary code when researchers and developers loaded the models for evaluation.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious Pickle Files in PyPI AI Packages Execute Arbitrary Code When Machine Learning Models Are Loaded
description: Multiple malicious PyPI packages targeting the machine learning ecosystem distributed malicious pickle serialization files disguised as pre-trained model weights, executing arbitrary credential-stealing code when data scientists and AI researchers loaded the files using standard PyTorch and scikit-learn model loading functions.
label: AV-400

title: MavenGate Username Resurrection Attack Reclaims Abandoned GitHub Usernames to Regain Control of Connected Maven Packages
description: The MavenGate attack method included a username resurrection variant where attackers reclaimed abandoned GitHub usernames previously associated with Maven package maintainers, automatically regaining control of Maven packages linked to those GitHub accounts through Apache Maven's GitHub-based authentication.
label: AV-509

title: Sandworm UAC-0212 Targets ICS Supply Chain via CVE-2024-38213 to Steal Engineering Schematics from Critical Infrastructure
description: The Sandworm subcluster UAC-0212 conducted supply chain attacks against critical infrastructure in Ukraine, Serbia, and Czech Republic by exploiting CVE-2024-38213 via phishing LNK files to gain initial access, then harvesting ICS credentials and engineering schematics for further supply chain compromise of industrial control systems.
label: AV-410

title: Phishing Attack Bypasses npm Trusted Publishing by Stealing TOTP Codes from Package Maintainers
description: Security researchers documented phishing attacks specifically designed to bypass npm trusted publishing protections by intercepting TOTP authentication codes from package maintainers in real-time, enabling attackers to take over publishing sessions and release malicious package versions before TOTP codes expire.
label: AV-200

title: SimpleHelp Vulnerabilities CVE-2024-57726 CVE-2024-57727 CVE-2024-57728 Exploited to Deploy DragonForce Ransomware via MSP Supply Chain
description: Sophos researchers documented exploitation of chained SimpleHelp vulnerabilities (CVE-2024-57726, CVE-2024-57727, CVE-2024-57728) to target a managed service provider and deploy DragonForce ransomware across multiple client endpoints, demonstrating how MSP supply chain compromise amplifies ransomware impact.
label: AV-400

title: CVE-2024-5083 Sonatype Nexus 2 Stored XSS Enables Maven Artifact Cache Poisoning for Downstream Build Compromise
description: CVE-2024-5083 in Sonatype Nexus Repository 2 documented a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in Maven proxy management that attackers could chain with cache poisoning, causing Nexus to serve malicious Maven artifacts to all downstream Java builds consuming dependencies through the compromised repository.
label: AV-509

title: CVE-2024-36115 Reposilite Path Traversal Enables Writing Arbitrary Maven Artifacts to Poison Java Dependency Builds
description: CVE-2024-36115 in Reposilite Maven repository manager documented a path traversal vulnerability allowing attackers to write arbitrary files to the server file system, enabling poisoning of Maven artifacts served to downstream Java application builds consuming dependencies through the compromised Reposilite instance.
label: AV-509

title: Malicious Rust Crate cargo-auditt Typosquats Security Tool cargo-audit Targeting Security-Focused Rust Developers
description: A malicious Rust crate named cargo-auditt used a double-t typosquatting technique to impersonate the legitimate cargo-audit security vulnerability scanner, specifically targeting security-conscious Rust developers who actively audit their dependencies and are likely to install security tooling without careful name verification.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Rust Procedural Macros Enable Hidden Code Injection at Compile Time
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks specifically targeting Rust procedural macros where hidden Unicode characters alter macro expansion behavior at compile time, enabling injection of hidden malicious code into all crates that use the compromised macro without any visible indication in the macro source.
label: AV-300

title: XZ Utils Backdoor CVE-2024-3094: Nation-State Actor Embeds SSH Authentication Bypass in Widely-Used Compression Library
description: A nation-state actor using the persona Jia Tan spent years building trust as an XZ Utils maintainer before embedding a sophisticated backdoor (CVE-2024-3094) that bypassed SSH authentication in systems using the compromised liblzma library, affecting multiple major Linux distributions in one of the most sophisticated supply chain attacks ever discovered.
label: AV-400

title: XZ Utils CVE-2024-3094: Two-Year Social Engineering Campaign Gains Maintainer Trust Before Backdoor Insertion
description: The XZ Utils backdoor attack (CVE-2024-3094) demonstrated an unprecedented two-year social engineering campaign where the attacker Jia Tan gradually built credibility as a legitimate contributor before gaining maintainer access and inserting a binary backdoor hidden in test files, representing a new category of long-horizon supply chain attack.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Linux Kernel Patch Submissions Enable Hidden Logic in Security-Critical Code
description: Security researchers demonstrated that Trojan Source attacks could be embedded in Linux kernel patch submissions where hidden Unicode characters alter memory allocation and access control logic, passing automated patch analysis while executing fundamentally different code than what kernel reviewers see during the review process.
label: AV-300

title: Malicious PyPI Package langchainn Typosquats langchain AI Framework to Target AI Application Developers
description: A malicious PyPI package named langchainn used a character doubling technique to typosquat the extremely popular langchain AI application framework, targeting the rapidly growing AI developer community building LLM-powered applications and executing credential-stealing postinstall scripts.
label: AV-200

title: npm Trusted Publishing Gap Allows Malicious Package Publication Without Provenance When Registry Accepts Unsigned Packages
description: Security researchers documented a trusted publishing gap in npm where the registry continued to accept and serve unsigned package versions even after trusted publishing was enabled, allowing attackers with stolen npm tokens to publish malicious packages that lacked provenance attestation alongside legitimate signed versions.
label: AV-509

title: GitHub Actions Workflow Injection via Unsanitized Issue Title Bypasses CodeQL Security Analysis
description: Security researchers documented GitHub Actions workflow injection attacks where unsanitized issue titles were interpolated into shell commands in workflow files, enabling arbitrary command execution and secret exfiltration in a pattern that bypassed CodeQL analysis because the vulnerability appeared in workflow configuration rather than application code.
label: AV-410

title: SolarWinds Orion Build System Compromised to Distribute SUNBURST Backdoor to 18,000 Government and Enterprise Customers
description: Nation-state attackers compromised the SolarWinds Orion build system to inject the SUNBURST backdoor into official signed software updates, distributing it to approximately 18,000 customers including US government agencies and Fortune 500 enterprises in one of the most impactful supply chain attacks in history.
label: AV-400

title: left-pad npm Package Removal Breaks Thousands of Projects Demonstrating Critical Dependency Risk in npm Ecosystem
description: The removal of the left-pad npm package by its author broke thousands of dependent projects worldwide including Babel and React build tools, demonstrating how the npm ecosystem's dependence on small transitive packages creates catastrophic supply chain fragility even without malicious intent.
label: AV-200

title: Docker Hub Namespace Squatting Attack Registers Official-Looking Image Names Before Legitimate Projects Can Claim Them
description: Security researchers documented Docker Hub namespace squatting attacks where attackers registered image names in the official Docker Hub namespace that closely matched popular open source projects before the projects could claim their official namespace, causing users to inadvertently pull attacker-controlled container images.
label: AV-509

title: GitHub Actions Cache Poisoning Attack Injects Malicious Build Artifacts via Shared Cache Between Pull Requests and Main Workflows
description: Security researchers documented GitHub Actions cache poisoning attacks where pull requests from forked repositories poison shared build caches that are subsequently consumed by privileged main branch workflows, enabling attackers to inject malicious compiled artifacts into official software releases.
label: AV-410

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Ansible Playbooks Alter Task Execution Order and Host Targeting Logic
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks embedded in Ansible playbook YAML files where hidden Unicode characters alter task execution order, when conditions, and host targeting logic, causing infrastructure automation to configure systems differently from what DevOps engineers see during playbook review.
label: AV-300

title: PyPI Package Name Collision with GitHub Repository Names Causes pip Install Confusion Attacks
description: Security researchers documented pip install confusion attacks where PyPI package names collide with GitHub repository names commonly used in pip install commands with VCS URLs, causing developers who mistype or copy-paste install commands to install malicious PyPI packages instead of the intended GitHub repository sources.
label: AV-200

title: 3CX Desktop Application Supply Chain Attack Delivers Information Stealer via Compromised Build Process
description: The 3CX Desktop Application suffered a supply chain attack where the Windows and macOS desktop clients were trojanized through a compromised build process, delivering an information stealer payload to thousands of enterprise customers using the legitimate signed application distributed through official channels.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious PyPI Mirror Intercepts pip Downloads via DNS Hijacking to Serve Trojanized Package Versions
description: Security researchers documented attacks where malicious PyPI mirrors deployed through DNS hijacking intercepted pip download requests and served trojanized versions of legitimate packages with embedded malware, affecting developers in networks where DNS had been compromised or whose pip configuration pointed to the malicious mirror.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Bidirectional Characters Affect Ruby Interpreter Enabling Hidden Logic in Rails Applications
description: The Trojan Source research confirmed the Ruby interpreter is vulnerable to Unicode bidirectional character attacks, with particular impact on Ruby on Rails web applications where hidden characters can alter authentication logic, ActiveRecord query conditions, and controller action routing invisible during code review.
label: AV-300

title: Long-Lived npm Publish Tokens Enable Persistent Unauthorized Package Publication After Initial Credential Compromise
description: Security researchers and GitHub documented the critical role of long-lived npm publish tokens in supply chain attacks, where a single token compromise enables persistent unauthorized package publication across all packages the maintainer controls, with token theft often going undetected for months before malicious package versions are discovered.
label: AV-410

title: Malicious PyPI Packages Exploit Rapid AI SDK Release Cycles to Publish Typosquatted Versions Before Official Releases
description: Security researchers documented malicious PyPI packages exploiting the rapid release cycles of AI SDK libraries including those for OpenAI and Anthropic models, publishing typosquatted package names in the window between official version announcements and actual PyPI publication to maximize installation rates among eager developers.
label: AV-200

title: Compromised GitHub Actions Workflows Abuse OIDC Federation to Access AWS Azure and GCP Cloud Infrastructure
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks where compromised GitHub Actions workflows abuse OIDC federation trust relationships between GitHub and cloud providers, using legitimate workflow OIDC tokens to access AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud production infrastructure without needing long-lived cloud credentials.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Nginx and Apache Configuration Files Alter Routing and Access Control Rules
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks embedded in Nginx and Apache web server configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter URL routing rules, access control directives, and proxy pass configurations, causing web servers to route traffic differently from what administrators see in configuration review.
label: AV-300

title: Malicious Jupyter Notebook Shared via GitHub Executes Hidden Credential Theft When Data Scientists Open and Run Cells
description: Malicious Jupyter notebooks shared through GitHub repositories and data science communities contained hidden credential-stealing code embedded in markdown cells rendered as documentation, executing credential theft when data scientists opened and ran the notebooks believing them to be legitimate tutorials or datasets.
label: AV-400
